questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. He works hard and is never downcast. B. He also brings strength of mind and industry in his life. C. He finds thousands of ways to clear an issue. D. A person with high determination can solve all his problems.
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} DCAB
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} ABCD
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} BCDA
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} CBAD
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct order is DCAB. Explanation: • Sentence D introduces the main idea, which is about a person with high determination being able to solve problems. • Sentence C supports this idea by stating that such a person finds many ways to clear an issue, elaborating on their determination. • Sentence A continues the thought by describing the person's character, mentioning that they work hard and maintain a positive attitude. • Sentence B concludes the paragraph by adding that the person brings strength of mind and industry to their life, which ties back to their determination and hard work. Thus, the correct order is DCAB.

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the option that correctly expresses the given sentence in passive voice. The workers built a new bridge across the river.
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} A new bridge is built by the workers across the river.

@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} A new bridge built across the river by the workers.

@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} A new bridge was built by the workers across the river.

@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} A new bridge will be built by the workers across the river.
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct option is: A new bridge was built by the workers across the river. Explanation: • The sentence is in the active voice: "The workers built a new bridge across the river." • To change it to passive voice, the object ("a new bridge") becomes the subject, and the subject ("the workers") comes after the verb with "by." • "Built" is the past tense of the verb, so the passive form should also be in the past tense: "was built."

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Parts of the following sentence have been given as options. Select the option that contains an error. While proceeding on / leave he had orally / committed that he will / resume after two days.
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} resume after two days
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} While proceeding on
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} committed that he will
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} leave he had orally
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct option containing an error is "committed that he will." Explanation: • The phrase "committed that he will" is incorrect in this context because of the tense mismatch. • The sentence is in the past tense ("he had orally committed"), so the reported speech needs to align with the past tense as well. Instead of using "will," which is future tense, it should be "would," which is the past tense form of "will" in reported speech. Correct sentence: "While proceeding on leave, he had orally committed that he would resume after two days."

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Arid
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} Wet
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Dry
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Humid
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Moist
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The most appropriate synonym for "Arid" is Dry. Explanation: • "Arid" means extremely dry, lacking moisture, or having little rainfall. • "Dry" directly conveys this lack of moisture, making it the best synonym. • "Wet" and "Humid" refer to moisture or moisture-heavy conditions, which are the opposite of arid. • "Moist" refers to a slightly damp or humid state, not as extreme as "dry," so it's not a strong synonym for "arid."

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the following sentence to fill in the blank. The room was spacious, but the closet felt ________.
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} Roomy
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Open
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Cramped
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Airy
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is: Cramped. Explanation: The word spacious means having a lot of space or roomy. The antonym of "spacious" is "cramped," which means confined or lacking sufficient space. Therefore, the sentence contrasts the spacious room with the cramped closet, making "cramped" the most appropriate choice. The other options ("roomy," "open," and "airy") are synonyms or related to spaciousness, not antonyms.

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate meaning of the phrase in bold. After losing his job, he decided to turn over a new leaf.
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} To repeat past mistakes
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} To ignore problems
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} To give up on life

@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} To start fresh and make positive changes
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The most appropriate meaning of the phrase "turn over a new leaf" is To start fresh and make positive changes. Explanation: • The expression "turn over a new leaf" means to begin anew, often with the intention of making better choices or improvements in one's life. • It doesn't mean repeating past mistakes, ignoring problems, or giving up on life. • It's about turning to a new chapter or trying to improve oneself, which aligns with starting fresh and making positive changes.

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the option that expresses the following sentence in active voice. The package will have been delivered by the courier before noon.
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} The courier will have delivered the package before noon.

@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The package will deliver the courier before noon.

@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The courier will deliver the package before noon.

@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The package will have delivered the courier before noon.
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is: (a) “The courier will have delivered the package before noon” Explanation: The original sentence is in passive voice: "The package will have been delivered by the courier before noon." • The subject of the sentence is the package, and the action (delivering) is being performed by the courier. • To convert it to active voice, the doer of the action (the courier) becomes the subject, and the receiver of the action (the package) becomes the object. • The verb tense (future perfect) remains the same: "will have delivered." Thus, the active voice version is: "The courier will have delivered the package before noon."

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the option that can be used as a one word substitute for the given group of words. A person who is excessively frugal or stingy with money.
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} Miser
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Spendthrift
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Altruist
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Philanthropist
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The one-word substitute for "a person who is excessively frugal or stingy with money" is Miser. Explanation: • A Miser is someone who hoards wealth and is extremely reluctant to spend money, even on things they need, because they are excessively frugal or stingy. • A Spendthrift is the opposite, someone who spends money freely and wastefully. • An Altruist is someone who selflessly helps others and doesn't focus on hoarding money. • A Philanthropist is someone who gives away large amounts of money to charitable causes, again the opposite of a miser.

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Choose the correct adverb to substitute the bracketed part in the sentence. She solved the math problem (most quickly) than her classmates.
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} quickest
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} more quicker
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} more quick
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} more quickly
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct adverb to substitute the bracketed part is more quickly. Explanation: • In comparative sentences, the adverb "quickly" should be used in its comparative form, which is "more quickly." • "Quickest" is the superlative form and is not appropriate for comparisons between two people. • "More quicker" and "more quick" are grammatically incorrect; "quicker" already serves as the comparative form of "quick," but in this case, we need the comparative form of the adverb "quickly," which is "more quickly."

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. 1. Stephen Hawking was a renowned theoretical physicist who made groundbreaking contributions to our understanding of black holes and the origins of the universe. 2. Despite being diagnosed with ALS at the age of 21, Hawking defied the odds and continued his research for decades, becoming an inspiration to millions 3. His work on Hawking radiation, which suggests that black holes emit energy, revolutionized the field of physics and earned him numerous accolades. 4. Hawking’s ability to communicate complex scientific ideas to the public, through books like A Brief History of Time, made him a global icon of science and perseverance.
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} 1243
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} 1432
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} 2431
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} 1324
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct order is 1 3 2 4. Explanation: • Sentence 1: Introduces Stephen Hawking and his contributions as a renowned theoretical physicist. • Sentence 3: Follows by highlighting one of his key contributions, Hawking radiation. • Sentence 2: Explains how, despite his ALS diagnosis, he continued his groundbreaking work and became an inspiration. • Sentence 4: Concludes by discussing his global fame and ability to communicate complex ideas, making him a science icon. So, the correct order is 1 3 2 4.

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the correctly spelt word.
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} Entrepreneur
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Enterpreneur
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Entreprenuer
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Enterprenuer
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correctly spelled word is Entrepreneur. Explanation: "Entrepreneur" is the correct spelling for a person who starts and manages a business, taking on financial risks in the hope of profit.

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} What does the idiom "Burn bridges" mean?
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} To start a fire unintentionally
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} To sever relationships irreparably
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} To learn a new skill
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} To be in a dangerous situation
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct meaning of the idiom "Burn bridges" is: To sever relationships irreparably. Explanation: • "Burning bridges" means to destroy a relationship or opportunity, making it impossible to go back or repair the damage. It usually refers to actions that end a connection in a permanent or destructive way.

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} In the following questions, a sentence has been given in direct/indirect speech. Out of the four alternatives suggested, select the one which best expresses the same sentence in indirect/direct speech. They said, "We were playing football when it started raining."
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} They said that they had been playing football when it started raining.

@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} They said that they were playing football when it started raining.

@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} They said that they are playing football when it started raining.

@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} They said that they had played football when it started raining.
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct option is: They said that they had been playing football when it started raining. Explanation: • The original sentence is in direct speech: "We were playing football when it started raining." • When converting to indirect speech, the past continuous tense ("were playing") changes to the past perfect continuous tense ("had been playing") to maintain the sequence of tenses. • The time reference ("when it started raining") remains unchanged because it is a subordinate clause describing the timing of the action.

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the following sentence to fill in the blank: The forest was teeming with wildlife, but the desert was ________.
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} Bustling
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Barren
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Abundant
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Swarming
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct antonym for "teeming" in this sentence is "Barren." Explanation: • "Teeming" means full of life or abundance, like the forest that is rich with wildlife. • Barren refers to a place that is empty or lacking life, which contrasts with "teeming." Let's look at the other options: • Bustling means full of activity, which might seem similar but is not the best opposite of "teeming" in this context. • Abundant means plentiful, which is similar to "teeming," not the opposite. • Swarming means large groups moving together, which also implies abundance and is not the opposite of "teeming." Therefore, "Barren" is the most appropriate antonym.

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Pensive
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} Carefree
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Thoughtful
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Happy
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Energetic
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The most appropriate synonym for Pensive is Thoughtful. Explanation: • Pensive refers to being deeply thoughtful, often in a serious or reflective manner. • Thoughtful also means being considerate and reflective, which aligns well with the meaning of "pensive." • The other options, such as Carefree, Happy, and Energetic, are all unrelated to the reflective or serious nature implied by "pensive." So, Thoughtful is the closest synonym to Pensive.

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. Strengthening the process of information sharing and enhancing liaison between counterterrorism and anti-drug agencies of the states is important. B. In this regard, the intelligence bureau has made a very important contribution in maintaining peace in the country since independence. C. Our country’s coastal security also has to be made impenetrable by keeping a close eye on even the smallest and most isolated port to make India more peaceful. D. India’s fight is against terrorism as well as its support system and unless it fights strictly against both of them, victory over terrorism cannot be achieved.
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} BCAD
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} BACD
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} DABC
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} CABD
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct order is DABC. Explanation: • Sentence D sets the context by discussing India’s fight against terrorism and its support systems, which introduces the main theme of the paragraph. • Sentence A follows logically by emphasizing the importance of strengthening information sharing and collaboration between agencies to combat terrorism and related issues. • Sentence B adds further depth by mentioning the contribution of the Intelligence Bureau in maintaining peace, supporting the idea of collaboration and intelligence-sharing. • Sentence C concludes the paragraph by highlighting the need for enhanced coastal security to ensure a peaceful country, tying it back to the broader issue of national security. Thus, the correct order is DABC.

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Parts of the following sentence have been given as options. Select the option that contains an error. Bangladesh has come / into existence / thirty-six years ago. / No error
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} Bangladesh has come
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} into existence
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} thirty-six years ago
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} No error
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The error is in "Bangladesh has come", and it should be "Bangladesh came". Explanation: • The phrase "has come" uses the present perfect tense, which is incorrect when referring to a specific event that happened at a defined point in the past (like "thirty-six years ago"). • The present perfect tense is used for actions that either have relevance to the present or have just been completed. Since "thirty-six years ago" points to a specific time in the past, the simple past tense should be used instead. Correct sentence: "Bangladesh came into existence thirty-six years ago."

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Choose the sentence with the correct use of a preposition:
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} She is good in playing tennis.
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} She is good at playing tennis.
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} She is good on playing tennis.
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} She is good for playing tennis.
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct sentence is She is good at playing tennis. Explanation: • The preposition "at" is used when referring to being skilled or proficient in a particular activity or task. So, the correct phrase is "good at" when talking about someone's ability in a specific area, such as playing tennis. • "In" and "on" are not used in this context for describing skills. • "For" would generally refer to purpose or suitability, which doesn’t fit when talking about proficiency in an activity. Thus, the correct sentence is: "She is good at playing tennis."

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the correct spelling to fill in the blank. His arguments were filled with _____, making it difficult to follow his logic.
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} contradictons
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} contradictions
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} contridictions
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} contradictons
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct spelling is contradictions. Explanation: • Contradictions refers to statements or ideas that oppose or conflict with each other, making it difficult to follow a logical argument or reasoning. So, the correct sentence is: His arguments were filled with contradictions, making it difficult to follow his logic.

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} In the following questions, a sentence has been given in direct/indirect speech. Out of the four alternatives suggested, select the one which best expresses the same sentence in indirect/direct speech. John said, "I am going to the market to buy some groceries."
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} John said that he is going to the market to buy some groceries.

@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} John said that he was going to the market to buy some groceries.

@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} John said that he had gone to the market to buy some groceries.

@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} John said that he will go to the market to buy some groceries.
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is John said that he was going to the market to buy some groceries. Explanation: In indirect speech, when the reporting verb (like "said") is in the past tense, we generally shift the tense of the reported speech one step back. This means: "I am going" (present continuous) changes to "he was going" (past continuous). So, "I am going to the market" becomes "he was going to the market" in indirect speech.

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Japan's involvement in World War II (1) ______ a significant impact on global history. The country, which had been rapidly industrializing, sought to expand its influence across Asia. In 1941, Japan (2) ______ a surprise attack on Pearl Harbor, which led to the United States entering the war. This decision (3) ______ by many as a turning point in the conflict. Despite initial successes, Japan faced severe setbacks as the war progressed. The atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki in 1945 (4) ______ the world and ultimately led to Japan's surrender. Today, the events of the war (5) ______ remembered as a reminder of the devastating consequences of conflict. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 1
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} have
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} has
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} had
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} having
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct option to fill in blank no. 1 is "had." Explanation: • The sentence refers to Japan's involvement in World War II and the impact it had on global history. The use of "had" is appropriate here because the event (Japan's involvement in World War II) occurred in the past and the impact it had on global history is being reflected upon from a past perspective. • "Had" is the correct past tense of the verb "have" to describe an action or event that happened in the past and had an effect during that time.

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Japan's involvement in World War II (1) ______ a significant impact on global history. The country, which had been rapidly industrializing, sought to expand its influence across Asia. In 1941, Japan (2) ______ a surprise attack on Pearl Harbor, which led to the United States entering the war. This decision (3) ______ by many as a turning point in the conflict. Despite initial successes, Japan faced severe setbacks as the war progressed. The atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki in 1945 (4) ______ the world and ultimately led to Japan's surrender. Today, the events of the war (5) ______ remembered as a reminder of the devastating consequences of conflict. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 2.
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} Penetrated
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Developed
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Escaped
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Launched
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct option to fill in blank no. 2 is "Launched." Explanation: The phrase "a surprise attack" suggests an action of initiating or starting something. "Launched" fits perfectly because it means to begin or carry out a planned attack. • "Penetrated" means to pass through something or break into it, which doesn't fit in the context of launching an attack. • "Developed" refers to something evolving or progressing, which doesn't fit the idea of a surprise attack. • "Escaped" means to get away from a situation, which doesn't make sense in the context of an attack. Therefore, the correct choice is "Launched", as it refers to the action of initiating a surprise attack. The correct sentence is: In 1941, Japan launched a surprise attack on Pearl Harbor.

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Japan's involvement in World War II (1) ______ a significant impact on global history. The country, which had been rapidly industrializing, sought to expand its influence across Asia. In 1941, Japan (2) ______ a surprise attack on Pearl Harbor, which led to the United States entering the war. This decision (3) ______ by many as a turning point in the conflict. Despite initial successes, Japan faced severe setbacks as the war progressed. The atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki in 1945 (4) ______ the world and ultimately led to Japan's surrender. Today, the events of the war (5) ______ remembered as a reminder of the devastating consequences of conflict. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 3.
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} was seen
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} is seen
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} were seen
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} are seen
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct option to fill in blank no. 3 is "Was seen." Explanation: • The sentence is talking about a past event and refers to how the decision was viewed at the time. Since it’s referring to a singular "decision," we use "was seen," the past tense, to indicate how the decision was perceived in the past. • "Is seen" and "are seen" are present tense forms, which would not be appropriate because the passage is discussing past events. • "Were seen" is used for plural subjects, but here "decision" is singular, so "was seen" is the correct choice. Thus, the correct sentence is: This decision was seen by many as a turning point in the conflict.

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Japan's involvement in World War II (1) ______ a significant impact on global history. The country, which had been rapidly industrializing, sought to expand its influence across Asia. In 1941, Japan (2) ______ a surprise attack on Pearl Harbor, which led to the United States entering the war. This decision (3) ______ by many as a turning point in the conflict. Despite initial successes, Japan faced severe setbacks as the war progressed. The atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki in 1945 (4) ______ the world and ultimately led to Japan's surrender. Today, the events of the war (5) ______ remembered as a reminder of the devastating consequences of conflict. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 4.
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} Suffocated
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Confused
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Shocked
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Amused
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct option to fill in blank no. 4 is "Shocked." Explanation: • The atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki in 1945 had a profound and devastating impact on the world. The word "shocked" appropriately conveys the strong emotional reaction of surprise and horror that people around the world would have experienced upon learning about the bombings. • "Suffocated" refers to difficulty in breathing or being deprived of oxygen, which doesn't fit the context of the bombings' global reaction. • "Confused" means being unsure or unclear, which doesn’t capture the intense emotional response to such catastrophic events. • "Amused" means finding something funny or entertaining, which is completely inappropriate in this context. Therefore, the correct sentence is: The atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki in 1945 shocked the world and ultimately led to Japan's surrender.

questions.question: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Japan's involvement in World War II (1) ______ a significant impact on global history. The country, which had been rapidly industrializing, sought to expand its influence across Asia. In 1941, Japan (2) ______ a surprise attack on Pearl Harbor, which led to the United States entering the war. This decision (3) ______ by many as a turning point in the conflict. Despite initial successes, Japan faced severe setbacks as the war progressed. The atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki in 1945 (4) ______ the world and ultimately led to Japan's surrender. Today, the events of the war (5) ______ remembered as a reminder of the devastating consequences of conflict. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no. 5.
options: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} is
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} are
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} was
@font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} were
questions.explanation: @font-face { font-family: 'Cambria Math'; src: url('https://appx-content-v2.classx.co.in/fonts/cambriamath.ttf') format('truetype');} *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct option to fill in blank no. 5 is "are." Explanation: • The phrase "Today, the events of the war" refers to a present-day reflection or remembrance of historical events. Since the sentence is in the present tense (referring to something that is still remembered today), we use "are" to maintain the correct subject-verb agreement. • "Is" is incorrect because it is singular, and "the events of the war" is a plural subject. • "Was" and "were" are in the past tense, which would not align with the present context of the sentence ("Today"). Therefore, the correct sentence is: Today, the events of the war are remembered as a reminder of the devastating consequences of conflict.
